issues.message: Formula uses volatile functions that can trigger expensive recalculation.
issues.rationale: Volatile functions recalculate on every workbook change, which can slow large models and make timing-dependent results harder to reason about.
issues.remediation: Replace volatile functions where possible, isolate them on a dedicated calculation sheet, or document why live recalculation is required.
issues.title: Volatile function

issues.rationale: Numeric literals embedded in formulas are harder to audit, reuse, and update than values stored in labeled input cells.
issues.remediation: Move constants into clearly labeled input cells or named ranges and reference those cells from the formula.
issues.title: Hardcoded numeric constant

issues.message: Formula uses volatile functions that can trigger expensive recalculation.
issues.rationale: Volatile functions recalculate on every workbook change, which can slow large models and make timing-dependent results harder to reason about.
issues.remediation: Replace volatile functions where possible, isolate them on a dedicated calculation sheet, or document why live recalculation is required.
issues.title: Volatile function

issues.message: Formula references an entire column range, which can slow recalculation.
issues.rationale: Entire-column references expand to very large ranges and can slow recalculation or hide accidental blanks in large worksheets.
issues.remediation: Restrict the range to the populated rows/columns, or convert the logic to a structured table with explicit bounds.
issues.title: Whole-column range reference

issues.rationale: The formula points at another workbook file, which makes results depend on external file paths, versions, and availability.
issues.remediation: Document the external dependency, confirm the linked file path, or paste values into a controlled input range when the model must be self-contained.
issues.title: External workbook reference

issues.rationale: The stored formula text still contains #REF!, so the workbook may calculate incorrectly even when neighboring cells look fine.
issues.remediation: Repair or remove the broken reference inside the formula, then recalculate downstream dependents after the source range is restored.
issues.title: Broken reference in formula

issues.rationale: Numeric literals embedded in formulas are harder to audit, reuse, and update than values stored in labeled input cells.
issues.remediation: Move constants into clearly labeled input cells or named ranges and reference those cells from the formula.
issues.title: Hardcoded numeric constant
